title: Addition & Subtraction
objective: Students will explore addition & subtraction concepts including Fluent adding and subtracting within 100, Fluent adding and subtracting within 20, Addition and subtraction within 1000 and more.

steps.content: Today we're going to learn about Fluent adding and subtracting within 100. Get ready for an adventure!
Fluently add and subtract within 100 using strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and the relationship between addition and subtraction
Time to try this out: Add two-digit numbers within 100 efficiently (e.g. 46 + 37 using place-value partitioning)
Can you reliably add or subtract two-digit numbers like '68 + 27' or '91 − 45', choosing the best strategy for each problem?

Great job! Today we learned about Fluent adding and subtracting within 100. Add two-digit numbers within 100 efficiently (e.g. 46 + 37 using place-value partitioning) Subtract two-digit numbers within 100 fluently (e.g. 83 − 25)
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Fluent adding and subtracting within 20!
Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies; know from memory all sums of two one-digit numbers
Let's practice together! Answer any single-digit addition fact within 3 seconds
Can you instantly recall all the single-digit addition facts — like '7 + 8 = 15' — without needing to count or think for more than a second?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Fluent adding and subtracting within 20. Answer any single-digit addition fact within 3 seconds Recall subtraction facts within 20 from memory (e.g. 15 − 8 = 7)
Let's discover the amazing world of Addition and subtraction within 1000!
Add and subtract within 1000 using concrete models, drawings, and strategies based on place value; understand composing and decomposing tens and hundreds
Let me show you something cool. Add two three-digit numbers using base-ten blocks or drawings, composing a ten or hundred when necessary
Can you add or subtract numbers in the hundreds — like '347 + 256' — by thinking about hundreds, tens, and ones separately, or drawing a diagram to help?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Addition and subtraction within 1000: Add two three-digit numbers using base-ten blocks or drawings, composing a ten or hundred when necessary Subtract three-digit numbers, decomposing a ten or hundred when necessary
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Adding and subtracting (age 7+)!
Add and subtract numbers with up to three digits using formal written methods of columnar addition and subtraction
Let's practice together! Set out a columnar addition correctly with digits aligned by place value
Can you set out a sum like '347 + 265' or '512 − 278' in columns and work through it step by step, carrying or borrowing where needed?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Adding and subtracting (age 7+): Set out a columnar addition correctly with digits aligned by place value Carry out columnar subtraction with exchange (borrowing) when needed
Today we're going to learn about Estimating by rounding. Get ready for an adventure!
Estimate the answer to a calculation and use inverse operations to check answers; apply to increasingly large numbers using rounding and inverse reasoning
Let's practice together! Round numbers to the nearest 10 or 100 to estimate a sum or difference before calculating
Before you works out a calculation, can they make a sensible rough guess — and then check their answer using the opposite operation?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Estimating by rounding: Round numbers to the nearest 10 or 100 to estimate a sum or difference before calculating Use addition to check a subtraction answer, or vice versa
Today we're going to learn about Missing number problems (age 7+). Get ready for an adventure!
Solve addition and subtraction problems including missing-number problems, using number facts, place value, and more complex methods
Let's practice together! Solve a missing-number problem such as 245 + ? = 380
Can you work out sums with a hidden number — like '? − 38 = 24' or '53 + ? = 91' — by thinking carefully about what the equation is asking?

Great job! Today we learned about Missing number problems (age 7+). Solve a missing-number problem such as 245 + ? = 380 Choose an appropriate method (mental, written, or combination) based on the numbers
Have you ever thought about Two-Step Word Problems? Let's find out together!
Solve one- and two-step word problems within 100 using addition and subtraction, with unknowns in all positions
Time to try this out: Solve a two-step word problem involving adding to and taking from
If a word problem says 'Sam had 78 stickers, gave some away, and now has 45 — how many did he give away?', can you work out which operation to use and find the answer?
Great job! Today we learned about Two-Step Word Problems. Solve a two-step word problem involving adding to and taking from Represent a word problem with an equation using a symbol for the unknown
Let's explore Addition and subtraction strategies (age 7+)! Have you ever wondered about this?
Explain why addition and subtraction strategies work, using place value and the properties of operations
Let's practice together! Explain why adding tens and ones separately gives the correct total
If you works out '67 + 24' by splitting it into '60 + 20' and '7 + 4', can they explain to you in words *why* that approach gives the right answer?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Addition and subtraction strategies (age 7+). Explain why adding tens and ones separately gives the correct total Describe why a compensation strategy works (e.g. 'I added 1 too many, so I subtract 1')
Let's explore Numbers on a number line! Have you ever wondered about this?
Represent whole numbers as lengths on a number line and represent sums and differences within 100 on a number line diagram
Time to try this out: Place whole numbers on a number line with equally spaced points
Can you mark numbers on a number line and show what happens when they add or subtract — for example, jumping forward 35 places from 48?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Numbers on a number line: Place whole numbers on a number line with equally spaced points Show an addition as a jump forward on the number line (e.g. 38 + 27 shown as jumps)
Today we're going to learn about Mental addition and subtraction (age 7+). Get ready for an adventure!
Mentally add and subtract a three-digit number and ones
Let's practice together! Calculate 345 + 7 mentally
Can you work out '436 + 7' or '521 − 8' in their head — keeping the hundreds and tens unchanged while adjusting just the ones?
You did it! Here's what we explored about Mental addition and subtraction (age 7+): Calculate 345 + 7 mentally Calculate 462 − 5 mentally
Today we're going to learn about Adding numbers. Get ready for an adventure!
Add up to four two-digit numbers using strategies based on place value and properties of operations
Let me show you something cool. Add three or four two-digit numbers by grouping tens and ones
Can you add up four two-digit numbers — like '14 + 23 + 32 + 19' — by grouping them cleverly, rather than just plodding through from left to right?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Adding numbers. Add three or four two-digit numbers by grouping tens and ones Look for pairs that make multiples of 10 to simplify addition
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Mentally adding hundreds to 3-digit numbers!
Mentally add and subtract a three-digit number and hundreds
Let me show you something cool. Calculate 345 + 200 mentally
Can you quickly work out '342 + 200' or '756 − 300' in their head — knowing that only the hundreds digit changes?
You did it! Here's what we explored about Mentally adding hundreds to 3-digit numbers: Calculate 345 + 200 mentally Calculate 762 − 400 mentally
Let's discover the amazing world of Mentally adding tens to 3-digit numbers!
Mentally add and subtract a three-digit number and tens
Time to try this out: Calculate 345 + 40 mentally
Can you quickly work out '354 + 40' or '628 − 30' in their head — knowing that only the tens digit changes?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Mentally adding tens to 3-digit numbers. Calculate 345 + 40 mentally Calculate 462 − 30 mentally
